Document:
# Roleplay Rule 6 - Don't metafriend.
Characters are allowed to have persistent relationships/friendships/rivalries across rounds, and remember information from previous rounds. However, in-character relationships can't be used to gain an unfair avdantage, and shouldn't devolve into metagrudging.
Additionally, don't just play only to interact with one specific person, and ignore everyone else.
## Precedents
- Character friendships should not be used to gain an unfair advantage. Having an IC friendship with another player does not, for example, justify giving them all-access each round. - Character rivalries doesn't justify metagrudging or [textlink="self-antagging" link="RuleSelfantag"]. - Character relationships does not justify cross-teaming, such as helping out a changeling.
